reset_tests.description: Drive rst=1 while sweeping clk and inputs; verify out stays combinational (15'b0 when in_valid=0, correct one-hot when in_valid=1) despite reset assertion
Deassert rst then reassert without changing in_valid/in; confirm no glitches or latching and clk phase is irrelevant

normal_tests.description: Exhaustive valid encodings with enable asserted: iterate in_valid=1 and in=0..14, expect a single out bit at position in
Enable gating check: for each in=0..14 hold in_valid=0 and confirm out remains all zeroes even when inputs change

corner_case_tests.description: Undefined in=15 handling: apply in_valid=1, in=15 and ensure design does not drive more than one bit or retain stale output (expect X/ignored but monitor for discrimination)
Simultaneous control extremes: in_valid toggles same cycle as rst; verify reset has no priority and output still purely combinational with in_valid gating

vectors.expected_out: 15'b00000100000?? (should remain zero if rst thinks it matters)

corner_case_tests.description: Boundary data patterns: alternate 0x0, 0xF, 0x5, 0xA to catch bit-order swaps or off-by-one indexing

sequence_tests.description: Glitch immunity over time: run multi-cycle pattern where in toggles every cycle, in_valid alternates 1/0, clk continues; confirm out instantly tracks current inputs with no state retention
Hold/setup edge case: change in immediately before clk edges with in_valid=1 to prove clk is irrelevant and outputs settle without registering

random_tests.description: Randomize in_valid (biased 60% high) and in (0-14 plus occasional 15) per cycle; check that out equals one-hot when valid and zero otherwise, flag any persistence across cycles

key_discriminators: in_valid=0 must force out=0 even if previous cycle had valid data—detects missing gating
clk/rst must have no effect on combinational encoder; any registered behavior exposes incorrect mutants
Exactly one out bit may be high for in_valid=1, in=0..14; multiple or shifted bits indicate decoding bugs
Undefined in=15 should not corrupt later outputs; lingering ones reveal stateful errors
